meaning: vt.呈递,归还,着色,汇报,致使,放弃,表演,实施vi.给予补偿n.交纳,粉刷,打底
examples: It's a concept that is difficult to render into English.
They couldn't resist anymore and decided to render up the city.
It contained so many errors as to render it worthless.
Procardia , a popular calcium channel-blocker sold generically as nifedipine , may render men infertile.
The FDA could render a verdict on Merck's Januvia by mid-October and Novartis' Galvus by December.
